commit.message:
Add tests for shared infrastructure and update documentation

Tests (25 new tests):
- CommandHelpersTests: subscription validation, timeframe validation,
  debug version printing
- OutputFormatterFactoryTests: factory returns all 6 formatters with
  correct types
- TextMarkdownFormatterTests: WriteRegions, WriteCostByTag,
  WritePricesPerRegion on both Text and Markdown formatters

Documentation:
- AGENTS.md: updated for net10.0, shared infrastructure (CommandHelpers,
  OutputFormatterFactory), coding conventions
- README.md: added prerequisites section, what-if devtest subcommand docs

CI:
- Enable coverage fail_below_threshold in dotnet.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve TypeScript build error in compare route by adding type-safe analysis parsing (#74)

Add enterprise-grade helper functions (parseAnalysis, extractAnalysisText,
createFallbackAnalysis) to safely normalize the AI response value from
Record<string, unknown> into a strongly-typed ParsedAnalysis object.

Handles all runtime shapes: already-parsed objects, JSON strings with
Markdown code fences, null/undefined, and primitive values.

* fix(infra): move CDK stacks from lib/ to stacks/, fix deploy script

- Rename infra/cdk/lib/ -> infra/cdk/stacks/ (lib/ was gitignored by root .gitignore)
- Update tsconfig.json: include stacks/, outDir: dist (was lib/)
- Update bin/app.ts: import from ../stacks/
- Exclude aws-sdk from Lambda bundle (Node18 runtime includes it)
- Fix deploy script: export AWS_VAULT_BACKEND, add --no-session flag

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(scripts): set GPG_TTY inside deploy script for pinentry-curses

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(infra): switch to aws-sdk v3, increase Lambda timeout/memory

- Replace aws-sdk v2 with @aws-sdk/client-dynamodb + @aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb (v3)
  Reduces cold start bundle from 9.3MB to 1.2KB
- Increase Lambda timeout: 3s -> 30s
- Increase Lambda memory: 128MB -> 256MB
- Default region in bin/app.ts: us-east-1 -> ap-northeast-1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(infra): deploy FastAPI UI via Mangum on Python Lambda

Replace Node.js CRUD handler with FastAPI app wrapped by Mangum.
Bundles app/, templates/, static/ into Lambda so the full UI is served.
Add binaryMediaTypes to API Gateway for CSS/JS responses.

Note: WebSocket (/ws/*) requires API Gateway WebSocket API and is
not supported in this REST API configuration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(scripts): deploy ApiStack first to resolve cross-stack export conflict

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(infra): add WebSocket API Gateway + Lambda + DynamoDB for real-time chat

- ws_handler.py: Lambda handler for $connect/$disconnect/$default routes
  Uses DynamoDB (ChaterConnections) for connection state, API Gateway
  Management API to send messages back to clients.
- websocket-stack.ts: CDK stack with DynamoDB table (RoomCodeIndex GSI),
  API Gateway WebSocket API, Python Lambda, IAM ManageConnections policy.
- api-stack.ts: inject WS_URL env var into HTTP Lambda for template rendering.
- app/main.py: pass ws_url from WS_URL env var into Jinja2 template context.
- templates/index.html: inject window.WS_URL via script tag when set.
- static/app.js: use window.WS_URL (API Gateway WSS) when set,
  fall back to local FastAPI WebSocket route for local development.
- docker-compose.yml: DynamoDB Local on port 8001 for local dev.
- scripts/init-dynamodb-local.sh: create ChaterConnections table locally.
- scripts/local_ws_server.py: local WS server wrapping ws_handler.py
  with DynamoDB Local, simulates API Gateway WebSocket behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: ignore infra/cdk/cdk.out from git

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use request.url_for() for static assets to resolve API Gateway stage prefix

Absolute paths like /static/style.css were missing the /prod stage
prefix when served through API Gateway, causing 403 errors.
Using request.url_for() lets Mangum's root_path resolve the correct URL.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: pass root_path to template for correct static asset URLs

Starlette's url_for() does not include root_path in generated URLs,
so API Gateway stage prefix /prod was missing. Instead, read root_path
from ASGI scope (set by Mangum) and pass it explicitly to the template.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: inject ROOT_PATH env var for static asset URL prefix

Mangum does not reliably expose root_path via ASGI scope.
Instead, CDK injects ROOT_PATH=/prod as env var so the template
generates correct /prod/static/... URLs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove unused import time in local_ws_server.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot CLI <copilot@example.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit - AI YouTube Bot

Fully automated YouTube + Instagram content creation bot.
- Viral topic detection (Twitter/X, Google Trends)
- AI script writing (Gemini)
- Voice narration (Edge-TTS)
- Stock footage (Pexels)
- Video assembly (MoviePy + FFmpeg)
- Auto-upload to YouTube + Instagram
- GitHub Actions for daily scheduled runs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Extract shared infrastructure and fix formatter implementations

- Create OutputFormatterFactory to eliminate 6-line formatter init duplication
- Create CommandHelpers with shared ValidateAndResolveSubscription(),
  ValidateTimeframe(), and PrintVersionIfDebug() methods
- Refactor all 11 commands to use shared helpers (net -267 lines)
- Add Validate() with subscription resolution to BudgetsCommand,
  RegionWhatIfCommand, and DevTestWhatIfCommand
- Fix .First() null guard in RegionWhatIfCommand (cache first item)
- Implement WriteRegions, WriteCostByTag, WritePricesPerRegion in
  TextOutputFormatter and MarkdownOutputFormatter (were NotImplementedException)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Deduplicate SCP ballot comparison and TTL simulation helpers

SCP: Make is_newer_prepare/is_newer_confirm pub(crate) in compare.rs and
call them from statements.rs instead of duplicating the logic.

RPC: Extract resolve_entry() helper shared by simulate_extend_ttl_op and
simulate_restore_op, eliminating duplicate key conversion, snapshot lookup,
and rent size computation.

Closes #1402
Closes #1413

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(terminal): use Windows-appropriate shell and PATH on win32

On Windows, SHELL is not set and /bin/bash does not exist. Fall back
to COMSPEC (cmd.exe) and skip the macOS/Linux PATH augmentation
(which uses Unix paths and the : separator).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix scraper: gender h1 extraction, slug detection fallback, canonical redirect handling

- Pass raw h1 text to _extract_gender so 'for women/men' span is present before brand/gender stripping
- Replace overly-broad fallback in get_perfume_urls (all /perfume/ links) with frequency-based slug detection — most common /perfume/X/ slug on the page is the brand's actual slug (handles cases like Dior vs Christian-Dior)
- Add canonical URL validation on brand pages to detect CF blocks (homepage redirect) vs legitimate designer renames (e.g. /designers/Christian-Dior.html → /designers/Dior.html); for renames, update slug from canonical and continue
- Fix seed_brands.json: update Christian Dior URL to /designers/Dior.html (correct canonical); remove duplicate Bulgari entry (was at index 15 and 95, both /designers/Bvlgari.html)

commit.message:
Switch payment provider from Stripe to PayPal

- Updated backend to use PayPal API instead of Stripe
- Replaced Stripe webhooks with PayPal webhooks
- Created PayPal order creation endpoint
- Updated .env to use PayPal credentials
- Removed Stripe dependency from package.json
- Added PAYPAL_SETUP.md with credentials guide

PayPal works in India without invite and supports international payments.
User can use their existing PayPal account.

commit.message:
feat(architecture): codebase sync — metrics stubs + AXIS comment

Section 6: Add src/api/metrics.ts with 10 canonical Prometheus metric
names per System Architecture FINAL Part 5 (Observability). Uses
existing ApprovalMetrics infrastructure; no new dependencies.

Section 8: Add AXIS Credential Vault architectural comment to
src/logicbridge/index.ts documenting boot position 6 and the current
embedded-in-LOGICBRIDGE implementation.

Canonical metric names (LOCKED per architecture doc):
  archieve_log_duration_ms, archieve_wal_depth,
  archieve_chain_verifications_total, archieve_sqlite_busy_total,
  seal_failure_total, governance_pipeline_duration_ms,
  dispatch_failure_total, gex_total (stub — GEX not yet implemented),
  idempotency_conflict_total, approval_pending_age_seconds

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: fix architecture.md + vision-tool-dev SKILL drift

architecture.md:
- Expand BRAIN layer to list all 9 providers
- Add all providers to table (DeepSeek, Groq, Mistral, Gemini, Anthropic, xAI)
- Server→Client: add token, stream_start, stream_finalize, thinking, key_saved,
  voice_settings, el_agent, partial_transcript message types

commit.message:
feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v6 suite with k-means filters and 30k scaling

Build conv_cifar_v6 suite (11 configs) exploring three new directions:

1. K-means filters as alternative to PCA — k-means centroids with
   triangle activation (Coates & Ng 2012) capture diverse patch
   prototypes rather than top-variance directions.  Test plain k-means
   and ZCA-whitened k-means at 10k/20k with ensembles.

2. PCA + per-patch contrast normalization — normalize_patches removes
   brightness variation at each spatial position, focusing PCA on
   texture/edge patterns.  Previously only tested with GCN (which hurt).

3. 30k ×10 scaling — v5 showed 30k ×5 hits 77.88% (new record).
   Test whether ×10 continues improving at 30k with α=2e-2.
   Also try α=1.5e-2 at ×5 (between 1e-2 and 2e-2 sweet spot).

v5 findings driving these choices:
- ×20 saturated at 20k (77.71% ≈ 77.68% at ×10)
- More filters per branch (48×3, 64×3) didn't help
- Cutout hurt -1.2% (corrupts PCA features)
- Hierarchical 2-stage disappointed (74.62% best)
- 30k + α=2e-2 is the one lever still improving

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: warn when provider API key is missing, falling back to client key

Adds a WARNING log when PROVIDER_<NAME>_API_KEY secret is not set,
making it obvious why upstream returns 401.

fix(#160): move core memories from system prompt to RAG-only retrieval (#162)

* fix(#160): move core memories from system prompt to RAG-only retrieval

- Remove [Core Memories] block from buildSystemPrompt() in ChatViewModel
- Split MAX_SEARCH_DISTANCE into CORE_MAX_DISTANCE=0.55f and EPISODIC_MAX_DISTANCE=0.40f
  (core memories cast a wider net — deliberately added = higher recall priority)
- Add lastAccessedAt to MemorySearchResult for sort tiebreaking
- Update searchMemories() signature: coreTopK=10, episodicTopK=5 (separate budgets)
- RagRepository: sort core results by score DESC, lastAccessedAt DESC before token truncation
- Add framing instruction to [User Profile] in system prompt
- Update RagRepositoryTest mock signatures for new 3-arg searchMemories

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#160): address code review — episodicTopK guard + tiebreaker test coverage

- Guard episodicTopK <= 0 in searchMemories() to skip JNI call and avoid
  spurious 'no such table' logcat warnings on every RAG call
- Add coVerify to lock down coreTopK=10, episodicTopK=0 contract in RagRepository
- Add lastAccessedAt tiebreaker test: equal-score memories sorted by recency,
  lower-recency entry truncated when token budget is tight

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#160): correct tiebreaker test token budget (30→75)

maxTokens=30 was below the framing header cost (~44 tokens), causing both
memories to be truncated. maxTokens=75 admits exactly one result:
- Recent fact (score=0.9, lastAccessedAt=9000): fits, budget drops to 3
- Stale fact (score=0.9, lastAccessedAt=1000): 3-4=-1, breaks loop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: ensure deploy-dev and deploy-prod jobs create or update cluster (#1332)

* feat: add cluster create/update logic to deploy-dev and deploy-prod jobs

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devantler-tech/platform/sessions/bf03cddf-6ceb-4155-85f4-b4726029521b

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add GITHUB_ACTOR and GITHUB_TOKEN env vars to cluster create/update steps

Both ksail.dev.yaml and ksail.prod.yaml reference ${GITHUB_ACTOR} and
${GITHUB_TOKEN} in localRegistry.registry. GITHUB_TOKEN is not a default
GitHub Actions env var, so it must be set explicitly for the cluster
create/update step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: use ksail cluster info to check cluster existence before create/update

Use `ksail cluster info` to determine whether the cluster already exists.
If the cluster is found, run `cluster update`; otherwise run `cluster create`.
This avoids the fragile `create || update` pattern that masks unexpected
create failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove manual SOPS Age decryption secret step

KSail now handles creating the SOPS Age decryption secret in the cluster
during `cluster create` and `cluster update`, so the manual
`kubectl create secret` step is no longer needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): pass OMNI_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY to all Omni-provider ksail steps

The Omni provider requires the OMNI_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY environment
variable for all ksail commands. It was missing from:
- cd.yaml: deploy-prod 'Create or update cluster' step
- ci.yaml: deploy-dev 'Create or update cluster', 'Push manifests',
  and 'Trigger Flux reconciliation' steps

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): pass OMNI_ENDPOINT to all Omni-provider ksail steps

The Omni provider requires the OMNI_ENDPOINT environment variable.
Added it to all ksail steps in both cd.yaml (deploy-prod) and ci.yaml
(deploy-dev).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove K8s 1.35 GA feature gates from Talos config

UserNamespacesSupport and UserNamespacesPodSecurityStandards feature
gates have been removed in Kubernetes 1.35 (GA, locked on). Having them
in the kubelet config causes a panic on startup:
  panic: unrecognized feature gate: UserNamespacesPodSecurityStandards

The sysctl user.max_user_namespaces is retained as it is still required.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add /v2 to mirror registry fallback endpoints

When overridePath is true, containerd uses the endpoint URL path as-is.
Harbor proxy endpoints already include /v2 in their path, but the direct
fallback endpoints (e.g. ghcr.io) did not. This caused image pulls to
use incorrect paths like /siderolabs/kubelet/manifests/... instead of
/v2/siderolabs/kubelet/manifests/..., returning 404.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle Omni cluster create CNI readiness timeout

ksail cluster create for Omni clusters does not install CNI (Cilium)
during creation, causing the readiness check to time out (node stays
NotReady without CNI). The cluster IS created successfully in Omni.

Changed the create-or-update logic from:
  cluster update || cluster create
to:
  cluster update || (cluster create || true; cluster update)

This ensures that after a new cluster is created (even if the readiness
check times out), cluster update runs to install CNI and Flux.

Also replaced the previous cluster info-based approach since cluster
info only checks the K8s API (not Omni API), failing when the cluster
exists but the API is unreachable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add TODO to simplify create-or-update once ksail#3878 is resolved

Reference: https://github.com/devantler-tech/ksail/issues/3878

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: simplify create-or-update to use cluster info (ksail#3878 resolved)

ksail v6.4.5 implements provider-aware cluster info that queries
Omni API first, then Kube API. This enables the cleaner pattern:
  if cluster info; then update; else create; fi

Also bumps ksail-cluster action from v5.91.6 to v6.4.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* build: bump ksail-cluster action to v6.5.1

Includes fix for kubeconfig context name mismatch (ksail#3904)
and nil pointer prevention in Helm client (ksail#3911).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): replace static KUBE_CONFIG with dynamic omnictl kubeconfig fetch

The KUBE_CONFIG secret becomes stale when the Omni cluster is
recreated. Replace it with a dynamic kubeconfig fetch from the
Omni API via omnictl, which always returns valid credentials.

The kubeconfig step uses continue-on-error because the cluster
may not exist yet (cluster create handles kubeconfig itself).

Workaround for https://github.com/devantler-tech/ksail/issues/3922

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): add context rename fallback for omnictl kubeconfig

omnictl's --force-context-name flag is silently ignored when no
pre-existing kubeconfig exists (fresh CI runner). Fall back to
kubectl config rename-context to ensure the context name matches
what ksail expects.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): add SHA256 checksum verification for omnictl binary

Verify the downloaded omnictl binary against the release
sha256sum.txt before installing it, mitigating supply-chain
risk.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <nikolaiemildamm@icloud.com>
